neighborhoods.name: Barclay Farm
neighborhoods.description: Well-established residential community in central Cherry Hill with mature trees, swim club, and proximity to schools and shopping

housing.predominantType: Split-level and colonial single-family homes from the 1960s-70s

neighborhoods.character_notes: Active civic association. Community pool and tennis. Close to Cherry Hill Mall and Route 70 shopping corridor. PATCO Haddonfield station is a short drive.

sources.title: Numbeo Cost of Living Cherry Hill

neighborhoods.name: Kingston Estates
neighborhoods.description: Upscale residential neighborhood in the eastern section of Cherry Hill with larger lots, mature landscaping, and quiet cul-de-sacs

housing.predominantType: Large colonial and contemporary homes on half-acre or larger lots

neighborhoods.character_notes: One of Cherry Hill's premier neighborhoods. Near Cherry Hill East High School. Very car-dependent but well-positioned for NJ Turnpike and I-295 access.

neighborhoods.name: Erlton
neighborhoods.description: Walkable western Cherry Hill neighborhood adjacent to Haddonfield borough, with a small-town feel and PATCO train access

housing.predominantType: Cape Cod and ranch-style homes from the 1950s-60s, some townhouses

neighborhoods.character_notes: Walking distance to PATCO Haddonfield station for direct train to Philadelphia (15 minutes). Adjacent to charming downtown Haddonfield with shops and restaurants.

neighborhoods.name: Cherry Hill Mall Area
neighborhoods.description: Central commercial zone surrounding the landmark Cherry Hill Mall, with diverse dining, retail, and a mix of housing options

housing.predominantType: Apartment complexes, condos, and townhouse communities

neighborhoods.character_notes: Cherry Hill Mall was one of the first enclosed malls in the eastern US. Strong Korean and Indian restaurant scene on Route 70. NJ Transit bus connections.

neighborhoods.name: Springdale
neighborhoods.description: Residential neighborhood in southern Cherry Hill near Springdale Road, with a mix of established homes and access to county parks

housing.predominantType: Ranch and bi-level homes from the 1960s-70s with yards

neighborhoods.character_notes: Near Croft Farm, which offers arts programs, trails, and community events. More affordable than northern Cherry Hill neighborhoods. Good access to I-295.
